jokes.text: I am having a love affair with this ice cream sandwich. Want some? You wanna take a bite? Take a bite.
jokes.setup: George Sr. is in prison and Michael is trying to discuss serious business matters
jokes.punchline: George Sr. is completely oblivious, romantically obsessed with an ice cream sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Sr.'s complete disconnect from reality. The escalating romantic language about a prison ice cream sandwich is both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No touching!
jokes.setup: Father and son having an emotional moment about family business
jokes.punchline: Prison guard interrupts with strict rule enforcement
jokes.explanation: Establishes a running gag that becomes iconic. Perfect economy and timing, interrupting emotional moments with bureaucratic absurdity.

jokes.text: I haven't had a vacation in years. This is my vacation. I'm exercising. I'm sleeping well. You're doing time. I'm doing the time of my life!
jokes.setup: Michael points out that George Sr. is in prison, not on vacation
jokes.punchline: George Sr. turns 'doing time' into 'time of my life' with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that reveals character's delusional optimism. The pun feels earned through George Sr.'s established personality.

jokes.text: He's a flamer.
jokes.setup: George Sr. introduces his cellmate T-Bone
jokes.punchline: Given the later arson context, 'flamer' has literal meaning beyond the slang
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning that pays off later. Works as casual slang initially but becomes literally accurate when T-Bone's arson is revealed. Pure AD layered writing.

jokes.text: Because he's a flamer? I told you that in confidence.
jokes.setup: Michael refuses to hire T-Bone due to no money
jokes.punchline: George Sr. thinks Michael is discriminating based on the 'flamer' comment
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that deepens the misunderstanding. George Sr.'s obliviousness to Michael's actual concerns drives the comedy.

jokes.text: Okay, we are just about ass to ankles back here, Maeby. Do you wanna hop on your cousin's lap there, please?
jokes.setup: Crowded car situation with teenage cousins
jokes.punchline: Michael's innocent suggestion creates maximum awkwardness given George Michael's crush
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy. Michael's obliviousness to George Michael's feelings creates perfect uncomfortable humor. The visual description 'ass to ankles' adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Whoa, bumpy road ahead.
jokes.setup: Just suggested Maeby sit on George Michael's lap
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely innocent traffic observation becomes unintentionally inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up that compounds the awkwardness. Michael's innocent road commentary becomes accidentally suggestive, perfect layering of cringe.

jokes.text: Welcome aboard, Mr. Manager. Wow! I'm Mr. Manager! Well, manager. We just say 'manager.'
jokes.setup: Michael promotes George Michael to manager of banana stand
jokes.punchline: George Michael's excitement over the redundant title, immediately deflated by correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's earnestness and Michael's mild exasperation. The redundant title and immediate correction creates a sweet, awkward rhythm.

jokes.text: Don't look at me, Mr. Manager. Right. It's up to me now. I'm Mr. Manager. Manager. We just say... I know, but you just... It doesn't matter who.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking if he needs an employee
jokes.punchline: The Mr. Manager correction becomes a confused loop, ending in Michael's frustrated surrender
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the confusion. Michael's defeat adds to the character dynamic showing his patience wearing thin.

jokes.text: I don't know what I expected.
jokes.setup: Michael finds Gob and Lindsay exactly where he left them that morning
jokes.punchline: Michael's perfectly deadpan acknowledgment of his family's predictable uselessness
jokes.explanation: Iconic AD line. Michael's weary resignation perfectly encapsulates the show's tone and his relationship with his family. Became a meme for good reason.

jokes.text: The only thing I found in the freezer was a dead dove in a bag. You didn't eat that, did you? 'Cause I've only got a couple days left to return it.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about not finding food while shopping
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dead dove in freezer with concern only about return policy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and dark. The casual treatment of a dead pet as a returnable item reveals character priorities and the family's dysfunction.

jokes.text: Died right in the middle of a show. Do you want a cage for that? No. I'm a magician.
jokes.setup: Context about the dead dove
jokes.punchline: Gob's matter-of-fact delivery about professional mishap and identity
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Gob's incompetent magician persona. The deadpan delivery of professional failure is nicely done.

jokes.text: You spent $68 on hair conditioner? A small price to pay for self-esteem, Michael. Or are you still jealous that you lost Best Hair to me in high school and got Dorkiest?
jokes.setup: Michael questioning Lindsay's expensive purchases
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's casual revelation of Michael's embarrassing high school superlative
jokes.explanation: Solid character dynamic establishment. The specific superlatives feel real and add to sibling rivalry history.

jokes.text: I mean, look at me. I'm an actor. An actor, for crying out loud! You know how much rejection I face every day?
jokes.setup: Tobias giving advice about not tying self-esteem to appearance
jokes.punchline: He claims to face rejection daily as an actor, but we know he never auditions
jokes.explanation: Sets up Tobias's delusion about his acting career. The dramatic delivery contrasts with his complete inactivity.

jokes.text: But you've never actually had an audition. Well... excuse me!
jokes.setup: Tobias giving inspirational speech about rejection in show business
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's brutal reality check followed by Tobias's wounded response
jokes.explanation: Good puncturing of Tobias's delusions. Lindsay's directness cutting through his pompous speech works well.

jokes.text: Yeah. Shoot me when that happens. Is there a carbon monoxide leak in this house?
jokes.setup: Family argument about setting examples for Maeby
jokes.punchline: Maeby's dry response and Michael's frustrated explanation attempt
jokes.explanation: Maeby's sarcasm and Michael's bewildered response to family dysfunction. Good character voice for Maeby.

jokes.text: You stay on top of her, buddy. Do not be afraid to ride her. Hard.
jokes.setup: Michael giving George Michael advice about supervising Maeby
jokes.punchline: Innocent work advice becomes accidentally sexual due to word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - completely innocent intent with unfortunate phrasing. The pause structure enhances the awkward impact.

jokes.text: I can't tell you how many health codes you're violating right now. I can't believe I volunteered for this. This is my stupidest rebellion ever.
jokes.setup: Maeby working at the banana stand
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on her rebellion being spectacularly mild
jokes.explanation: Good Maeby character moment. Her self-awareness about the pathetic nature of her 'rebellion' is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Easy. Banana. Buck. Banana. Take a buck.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining the banana stand accounting system
jokes.punchline: Maeby immediately starts embezzling with casual confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Maeby's immediate grasp of fraud opportunities. The rhythm and casual delivery make it funnier.

jokes.text: Then why don't you marry an ice cream sandwich?
jokes.setup: Phone conversation with George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Lucille's exasperated response to George Sr.'s continued ice cream sandwich obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the ice cream sandwich obsession continues. Lucille's exasperation feels earned.

jokes.text: You know, it's really more believable if you let me finish.
jokes.setup: Lucille denies knowing about flight records before Michael finishes asking
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation about her premature denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael line showing his exasperation with family incompetence. The meta-commentary on lying technique is clever.

jokes.text: Something with 'Dale.' I don't know. Brookfeather. Raintree. It's hot. It's very hot there. I've never been. Get a warrant!
jokes.setup: Michael asking for storage unit location
jokes.punchline: Lucille's increasingly frantic attempts to seem ignorant while clearly knowing details
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Lucille's terrible lying skills. The details she 'doesn't know' clearly indicate she knows exactly where it is. The temperature complaint seals it.

jokes.text: Something with 'Dale.' I don't know. Brookfeather. Raintree. It's hot. It's very hot there. I've never been. Get a warrant!
jokes.setup: Michael pressing Lucille for storage unit location
jokes.punchline: Lucille's increasingly panicked and contradictory responses ending in legal defiance
jokes.explanation: Good escalating panic from Lucille. The contradictory details and sudden legal stance create a nice comedic build.

jokes.text: Luz, that coat cost more than your house! Oh, that's how we joke. She doesn't even have a house.
jokes.setup: Lucille scolding her housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Attempting to soften the cruelty but making it worse by revealing homelessness
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Lucille moment. The attempt to soften cruelty that actually makes it worse is perfect character writing and shows her complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Oh, look at all these parts!
jokes.setup: Tobias continues looking at classified ads
jokes.punchline: Sees auto parts listings as acting roles
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Tobias misinterpreting everything as acting-related. Shows his complete disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Oh, look at all these parts!
jokes.setup: Tobias looking through acting classifieds
jokes.punchline: Tobias's excitement over job listings, oblivious to his lack of qualifications
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Tobias's delusional optimism about his acting prospects.

jokes.text: Am I panicked about the fire, or am I being brave for everyone else? The fire... It's... It's a fire sale. Oh. Oh. Okay, I didn't, um... Well, let's give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Tobias preparing for commercial audition
jokes.punchline: Tobias doesn't understand it's a retail sale, thinks it's about an actual fire emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Tobias's over-acting. His genuine confusion about the concept shows his disconnect from normal commercial work.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! We're having a fire... sale. Oh, the burning! It burns me! Evacuate all the schoolchildren! This isn't a fever! I can't even see where the knob is!
jokes.setup: Tobias's misunderstanding of 'fire sale' concept
jokes.punchline: Completely over-the-top performance treating it as actual emergency
jokes.explanation: Iconic AD moment. Tobias's complete commitment to the wrong interpretation creates perfect physical comedy. The escalating panic and specific details make it memorable.

jokes.text: Would you like to try that a little simpler maybe? No.
jokes.setup: Casting director's reasonable request for toned-down performance
jokes.punchline: Tobias's flat refusal to compromise his 'artistic vision'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment. His complete lack of professional flexibility delivered with such certainty is both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Somebody wanted this place to go. Yeah? What do you mean, arson? Oh, definitely the work of a flamer.
jokes.setup: Fire chief explaining the storage unit fire
jokes.punchline: Fire chief unknowingly confirms George Sr.'s earlier description of T-Bone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the earlier 'flamer' setup. The double meaning becomes literal and the fire chief unknowingly confirms the slang usage. Perfect AD layered writing.

jokes.text: I think I made the fire too real for them and probably failed to highlight the sale.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining why he didn't get the commercial
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands why his performance was problematic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias self-analysis - he thinks his failure was being too good at acting rather than completely inappropriate. Shows his delusion while being technically accurate.

jokes.text: Well, that's great, but I've got my own problems. The flight records? They burned up. That's fine. That's over with. But I think Dad is behind it.
jokes.setup: Lindsay celebrating her job booking while Michael has serious problems
jokes.punchline: Michael's mounting realization that his father orchestrated the evidence destruction
jokes.explanation: Solid plot development moment. Michael's growing awareness creates good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: But you don't have a job. You've got a job offer. Anybody can get a job offer.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming she's making a fortune at her new job
jokes.punchline: Michael's brutal reality check about the difference between offers and employment
jokes.explanation: Good Michael deflation of family delusions. His precision in pointing out the distinction feels earned.

jokes.text: I can't trust a mailman with this. This is important.
jokes.setup: Michael asking why Gob can't give letter to mailman
jokes.punchline: Gob's paranoid importance about what's likely a mundane letter
jokes.explanation: Sets up Gob's dramatic gesture later. His false sense of importance about simple tasks is good character work.

jokes.text: The banana stand! You know, that's exactly what this feels like. Huh? Like I'm still stuck in that hot booth all summer with you pulling the strings.
jokes.setup: George Sr. keeps mentioning money in the banana stand
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustrated realization about his ongoing manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's awareness of his father's manipulation patterns. Sets up the burning decision.

jokes.text: We throw away a banana for every buck we take so no one finds out. Wait a minute. I think you should do that math again. Why? Is it wrong? It's fine. He's an arsonist, not an embezzler.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining their embezzlement system
jokes.punchline: George Michael's gentle correction and resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic. George Michael's politeness about correcting criminal activity and his final resigned comment are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Gob had not mailed the letter, but, in an act of defiance... dramatically hurled the letter into the sea. This proved a more difficult dramatic gesture than he'd anticipated.
jokes.setup: Gob decides to dramatically dispose of the letter
jokes.punchline: Visual of Gob struggling to throw letter into ocean from pier
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy setup by narrator. The contrast between Gob's dramatic intention and pathetic execution is classic AD physical humor.

jokes.text: This proved a more difficult dramatic gesture than he'd anticipated.
jokes.setup: Gob dramatically hurls the letter into the sea
jokes.punchline: Narrator's understated description of Gob struggling with the throw
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development - deflating a dramatic moment by revealing the practical difficulty. The narrator's dry tone makes Gob's failure funnier.

jokes.text: You want your belt to buckle, not your chair.
jokes.setup: Family dinner at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Lucille's cutting comment about someone's weight/posture
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille cruelty with clever wordplay. The specific imagery makes it memorable and perfectly captures her character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you're right. I am? Oh, God, I've screwed this up! Okay, we gotta go.
jokes.setup: Maeby points out they're losing twice as much money as they thought
jokes.punchline: George Michael's brief pride followed by immediate panic
jokes.explanation: Good George Michael moment. His momentary pride in being right immediately replaced by panic is endearing.

jokes.text: Pop-pop, he's a business man. What would he do? Bananas Foster.
jokes.setup: Kids panicking about getting caught embezzling
jokes.punchline: Maeby's solution to burn the evidence, disguised as dessert order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that serves multiple purposes - dessert order, arson plan, and character trait. The double meaning is expertly crafted and feels completely natural.

jokes.text: That's funny, 'cause I was gonna say, 'You might wanna lean away from that fire... since you're soaked in alcohol.' Mine was better.
jokes.setup: Competitive banter between mother and daughter
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's smug satisfaction with her comeback
jokes.explanation: Decent family dynamic moment. Shows the competitive nature of their relationship, though the joke itself is fairly standard.

jokes.text: Welcome to Bluth Bananas, where bananas are our business. May I interest you in a banana this day?
jokes.setup: T-Bone working at the banana stand
jokes.punchline: His overly formal sales pitch for frozen bananas
jokes.explanation: T-Bone's attempt at professional salesmanship for a simple frozen banana stand. The formality contrasts with the casual beach setting.

jokes.text: Welcome to Bluth Bananas, where bananas are our business. May I interest you in a banana this day?
jokes.setup: Professional criminal working at banana stand
jokes.punchline: T-Bone's overly formal customer service approach
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing T-Bone's incongruous formality. The contrast between his criminal nature and customer service enthusiasm works.

jokes.text: Michael, having a nice day at the beach while the rest of us are busting our asses to deliver your mail?
jokes.setup: Gob confronting Michael at the beach
jokes.punchline: Claims to be working hard while clearly relaxing at the beach himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob hypocrisy - accusing Michael of leisure while doing exactly the same thing. His indignation despite his own failure is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Better than being treated like the goofball... the joker, the magician. I thought you were gonna do, like, a trick there. A fireball or something. I was. It didn't go off. These things never go off when you want them to. My least consistent trick.
jokes.setup: Gob lamenting his treatment by the family
jokes.punchline: Failed magic trick demonstrates his incompetence perfectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment. His complaint about being seen as incompetent is immediately proven by his incompetent magic. The self-aware admission of inconsistency is beautifully timed.

jokes.text: I thought you were gonna do, like, a trick there. A fireball or something. / I was. It didn't go off. These things never go off when you want them to. My least consistent trick.
jokes.setup: Michael expecting Gob to do magic during their argument
jokes.punchline: Gob admits his magic tricks are unreliable
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's professional incompetence even in his chosen field. His casual admission of failure while maintaining his magician identity is perfect.

jokes.text: I was just, uh... I was burning down the banana stand.
jokes.setup: Michael catches George Michael at the burning banana stand
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact confession to arson
jokes.explanation: George Michael's honest, casual admission to a felony shows his guilt and innocence simultaneously. The understated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I was just, uh... I was burning down the banana stand. What? I'm sorry. I screwed it all up. I've got no right to call myself Mr. Manager. Manager.
jokes.setup: Michael catches George Michael about to burn down the stand
jokes.punchline: George Michael's honest confession and continued title confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier title confusion. George Michael's earnest self-punishment and continued title error is endearing.

jokes.text: Burn it down. What? Let's burn this son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: George Michael confessing to planned arson
jokes.punchline: Michael's unexpected approval and encouragement
jokes.explanation: Great reversal moment. Michael's sudden shift from responsible parent to enabler shows character growth and creates a bonding moment. The profanity from Michael is surprising and effective.

jokes.text: You mailed that insurance check, right, Gob?
jokes.setup: Brothers enjoying burning down the banana stand
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual assumption about the insurance, knowing Gob's unreliability
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony. The audience knows Gob threw the letter away, making Michael's casual assumption devastating. Great setup for the final revelation.

jokes.text: There was $250,000 lining the walls of the banana stand. What? Cash, Michael. Why didn't you tell me that? How much clearer can I say... 'There's always money in the banana stand'?
jokes.setup: Michael proud of burning down the stand
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s revelation about literal money hidden inside
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the running line. The literal interpretation of the metaphor creates perfect dramatic irony and devastation. This is masterful sitcom writing that rewards attention to detail.

jokes.text: How much clearer can I say 'There's always money in the banana stand'?
jokes.setup: Michael's shock at the money revelation
jokes.punchline: George Sr. points out he literally told Michael this multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that shows George Sr. was being completely literal all along. The audience's assumption about metaphorical meaning creates the comedy.

jokes.text: No touching! No touching! No touching!
jokes.setup: Father and son having emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Multiple guards enforcing the no touching rule
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag. The multiple voices emphasizing the rule at their moment of despair adds to the absurdist bureaucracy theme.

jokes.text: Return from whence you came!
jokes.setup: Gob at pet store with frozen dove
jokes.punchline: Dramatic proclamation to dead bird
jokes.explanation: Good Gob absurdity. His theatrical approach to a return policy dispute with a dead animal is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You couldn't have called me? I can't swing a hammer? I got a rabbit to buy.
jokes.setup: Michael and George Michael rebuilding banana stand without Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob's hurt feelings and random rabbit comment
jokes.explanation: Good Gob character moment. His wounded pride mixed with random magic supply needs is appropriately scattered.
